S-PRIVACY-IR.notes: privacy_ir.rs: canonical typed set-intersection, private-lookup, and aggregate operations admit PSI/PIR/VDAF/MPC/TEE/FHE/ZK lowerings only when backend-specific named trust roots, semantic leakage and composition budgets, workload key holders/epochs, fail-closed recovery, and non-guarantees are explicit. A separate reference interpreter and lowered interpreter agree for every admitted backend; UI summaries preserve trust/non-guarantee names. Falsifiers reject unsupported downgrade, hidden TEE/committee trust, underdeclared/composed leakage, permanent global keys, duplicate roots, and operation/shape/backend ambiguity. This implements the local IR/admission claim, not any backend cryptosystem's security.

M-HFHE-SUITE.notes: hfhe.rs + refresh.rs: exact deterministic F_(2^127-1) arithmetic and shape-bound ciphertext-as-circuit encoding are implemented as a conformance/cryptanalysis precursor, while the prior Z/(2^64) LWE-shaped ciphertext is explicitly a negative control. The boundary always reports registered=false/enabled=false and local activation rejects. Wrong-field downgrade, gate splice, noncanonical field, ciphertext shape ambiguity, unsupported suite dispatch, and execution-checked plaintext discontinuity reject. The row stays PARTIAL: no IND model, standard-assumption reduction, independent concrete attack estimate, production parameters, complete public refresh prover, two independent verifier families, or rotation evidence exists.

M-PROOF-CARRYING-REFRESH.notes: refresh.rs: dispatch binds proof suite, source/destination suites, both epochs, source/destination rights and policy roots, chain, job, transcript, fresh challenge window, both shape-bound ciphertext commitments, and plaintext continuity digest. Stateful verification rejects missing/stale/replayed challenges, epoch rollback, suite/policy/rights/context mutation, ciphertext substitution, proof tamper and cross-transcript splice; retired secret/audit keys expose an explicit zeroized state and reject use. A separate deterministic local execution backend reopens the pinned negative-control ciphertexts and rejects plaintext discontinuity even when an audit-key holder recomputes an otherwise valid tag. The row stays PARTIAL because that backend uses the master secret and is neither zero knowledge nor independently authored; the public proof remains a symmetric audit-key tag over a toy negative-control ciphertext.

N-TEE-FIBER.notes: tee_fiber.rs integrates against noos-nel PromptJob/PrivacyProfile/FinalityClass public interfaces and defines a hardware-quote verifier boundary binding encrypted prompt commitment, exact job/model/decoding/numeric profile, executor identity/binary, firmware/vendor trust, policy, output, fresh challenge, expiry, monotonic rollback counter, and a host-selected verifier family distinct from quote output. Receipts are exactly ASSURED_TEE, name vendor/verifier trust, and reject every NEL open/assured/proven settlement class. Leakage admission binds a preregistered suite, distinct prompt/trial commitments, equal public behavior, minimum power, and length/duration budgets. Fail-closed rollback records an immutable route, preserves historical receipts, disables new jobs, and cannot be overwritten. The row stays PARTIAL because the verifier fixture is not real vendor hardware and no preregistered real-hardware timing/length campaign or confidential arbitration evidence exists.

E-NEL-08.notes: tee_fiber.rs provides executable local falsifiers for complete attestation mutation, relay across job/model/profile, challenge replay, stale expiry, monotonic-counter rollback, firmware downgrade, cross-job prompt separation, equal-public-behavior length/duration-bucket budget accounting, and attempts to spend TEE receipts as NEL Soft/Anchored/Assured/Proven. All reject or remain inside the zero-delta local fixture budget, and rollback disables only the private profile. The row stays PARTIAL: no real TEE, vendor, prompt side-channel timing campaign, public-duration evidence, or declared confidential-arbitration threshold/adversary has been exercised.

E-NEL-PRIVATE-01.notes: lib.rs + transformer.rs: deterministic small-transformer BESI precursor physically pads every remote public-weight GEMM to 128 rows, split-shares over Z/(2^64), reconstructs raw accumulators, derives two post-response transcript-bound fresh exact-Z Freivalds challenges, and slices/requantizes only after acceptance; nonlinear/attention/KV-equivalent/residual work remains local. An immutable trust-policy commitment names two distinct executor identities/channel roots, the fixed model/profile and registered adjudicator while explicitly retaining non-collusion as an assumption. Executor-view admission binds authenticated party/key source and exposes only bucket rows. Escrow recovery moves only on a fully tuple-bound signature by the registered key; replay, rebound context, attacker keys and post-freeze transitions reject. The row stays PARTIAL: the deterministic fixture is not OS-CSPRNG sharing or independent executors, and it does not regenerate the frozen real Qwen2.5-0.5B 8/8-GEMM, 25-test, timing, or transport evidence.
